section.heading: Obsolete references in existing law to basic pay rates

paragraph.content: the rate of pay of any employee or position, or class of employees or positions, the pay for whom or for which is disbursed by the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, or any maximum or minimum rate with respect to any such employee, position, or class, is referred to in or provided by statute or House resolution; and
the rate so referred to or provided is a basic rate with respect to which additional pay is provided by law;

section.continuation: such statutory provision or resolution shall be deemed to refer, in lieu of such basic rate, to the per annum gross rate which an employee receiving such basic rate immediately prior to the effective date of this section would receive, without regard to such statutory provision or resolution, under section 334 11 See References in Text note below. of this title on and after such date.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 334 of this title, referred to in text, was repealed by Pub. L. 104–186, title II, § 210(3)(A), Aug. 20, 1996, 110 Stat. 1743.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 335 of this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1996—Par. (1). Pub. L. 104–186 substituted “Chief Administrative Officer” for “Clerk”.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective immediately prior to noon on Jan. 3, 1971, see section 601(1) of Pub. L. 91–510, set out as an Effective Date of 1970 Amendment note under section 4301 of this title.
